svg.aria-label: A continuous calligraphic line drawing for Brazil. A single faint horizontal rule runs the width of the page like a baseline of years. Standing on it are three upright ink strokes that rise in height from left to right — a literacy figure climbing across three years, the tallest at the right. One continuous fine line traces their ascending tops. Beneath that climb, a second and fainter line follows the same rising shape held lower: the external audit that reads below the headline. At the right edge, alone, a short kiln-orange bracket measures the vertical gap between the two lines — the seven points between the number a system reports on itself and the number reported from outside.
svg.title: The Still · Brazil — a rising literacy figure, and the audit that reads below it
svg.desc: A single faint horizontal rule crosses the page like a baseline of years. On it stand three upright ink strokes that grow taller from left to right — an annual literacy figure climbing across three years, the steepest gain into the last. A continuous fine line traces their ascending tops, lower-left up to upper-right. A second, fainter line shadows that climb just beneath it, the same rising shape held lower: an externally-administered measure that reads below the headline one. At the right edge a short kiln-orange bracket, the only warm thing on the page, measures the gap between the two lines — the seven points between a number a system reports on itself and the number reported from outside. No filled volumes; the line is the image; the ground is paper-cream.
